_**Agent (default)**_

There is **nothing named “Stitch”** in your **OttStreamingApp** repo: no files or text matches for `stitch`.

So “list my stitch project” is almost certainly about **something outside this codebase**. Common meanings:

### 1. **MongoDB Stitch / Atlas App Services**
“Stitch” was renamed to **Atlas App Services** (same idea: serverless backend tied to MongoDB Atlas).

- **In the browser:** [MongoDB Atlas](https://cloud.mongodb.com) → your org/project → **App Services** (or “Apps”) to see app definitions.
- **CLI:** if you use the Atlas CLI, listing is done with Atlas/App Services commands after you’re logged in (exact subcommands depend on your CLI version).

I **cannot** see your Atlas org or list those apps from here (no access to your account).

### 2. **Another product or folder**
“Stitch” might be a **different app folder** on your machine, a **design handoff** tool, or an internal name. If you meant a **path** or **service name**, say which one.

---

**What I need from you to be precise**

- Do you mean **MongoDB Atlas App Services** (old Stitch), or something else?
- If it’s Atlas: are you trying to **list apps in the UI**, or **from the terminal** (and which OS/tool you use)?

I’m in **Ask mode**, so I can’t run commands that talk to your cloud account or change anything; I can only tell you **where and how** to list things once we know which “Stitch” you mean.

\\":\\\"#5b403e\\\",\\\"primary\\\":\\\"#ffb3ae\\\",\\\"primary_container\\\":\\\"#ff5351\\\",\\\"primary_fixed\\\":\\\"#ffdad7\\\",\\\"primary_fixed_dim\\\":\\\"#ffb3ae\\\",\\\"secondary\\\":\\\"#ffb3ae\\\",\\\"secondary_container\\\":\\\"#822625\\\",\\\"secondary_fixed\\\":\\\"#ffdad7\\\",\\\"secondary_fixed_dim\\\":\\\"#ffb3ae\\\",\\\"surface\\\":\\\"#131313\\\",\\\"surface_bright\\\":\\\"#3a3939\\\",\\\"surface_container\\\":\\\"#201f1f\\\",\\\"surface_container_high\\\":\\\"#2a2a2a\\\",\\\"surface_container_highest\\\":\\\"#353534\\\",\\\"surface_container_low\\\":\\\"#1c1b1b\\\",\\\"surface_container_lowest\\\":\\\"#0e0e0e\\\",\\\"surface_dim\\\":\\\"#131313\\\",\\\"surface_tint\\\":\\\"#ffb3ae\\\",\\\"surface_variant\\\":\\\"#353534\\\",\\\"tertiary\\\":\\\"#71d5e0\\\",\\\"tertiary_container\\\":\\\"#319ea9\\\",\\\"tertiary_fixed\\\":\\\"#8ef2fd\\\",\\\"tertiary_fixed_dim\\\":\\\"#71d5e0\\\"},\\\"designMd\\\":\\\"# Design System Strategy: The Cinematic Curator\\\\n\\\\n## 1. Overview \u0026 Creative North Star\\\\nThe North Star for this design system is **\\\\\\\"The Cinematic Curator.\\\\\\\"** \\\\n\\\\nIn a world of cluttered OTT interfaces, this system rejects the \\\\\\\"infinite grid\\\\\\\" in favor of a high-end editorial experience. We treat digital real estate like a luxury film program. To move beyond a \\\\\\\"template\\\\\\\" look, we leverage **intentional asymmetry**—such as oversized hero titles overlapping content cards—and a rigorous **tonal layering** strategy. This creates a sense of \\\\\\\"physical\\\\\\\" depth where content isn't just displayed; it is presented. The interface should feel like a darkened theater where only the most important elements catch the light.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### 2. Colors \u0026 Surface Architecture\\\\nOur palette is rooted in the \\\\\\\"Absolute Dark\\\\\\\" philosophy. We do not use pure black (#000) for surfaces to avoid \\\\\\\"crushing\\\\\\\" the visual depth. Instead, we use deep charcoals and warm bordeaux-tinted grays.\\\\n\\\\n#### The \\\\\\\"No-Line\\\\\\\" Rule\\\\n**Explicit Instruction:** Designers are prohibited from using 1px solid borders to define sections or containers. \\\\nBoundaries must be defined solely through:\\\\n1.  **Background Shifts:** Moving from &#96;surface&#96; (#131313) to &#96;surface_container_low&#96; (#1C1B1B).\\\\n2.  **Vertical Breathing Room:** Utilizing the &#96;8&#96; (2.75rem) or &#96;10&#96; (3.5rem) spacing tokens to signal a change in context.\\\\n\\\\n#### Surface Hierarchy \u0026 Nesting\\\\nTreat the UI as a series of stacked layers. The closer an element is to the user, the \\\\\\\"brighter\\\\\\\" its surface becomes:\\\\n*   **Base Layer:** &#96;surface&#96; (#131313) for the main application background.\\\\n*   **Sectional Layer:** &#96;surface_container_low&#96; (#1C1B1B) for large content groupings.\\\\n*   **Interactive Layer:** &#96;surface_container_highest&#96; (#353534) for cards, modals, or hovering states.\\\\n\\\\n#### Glass \u0026 Gradient Implementation\\\\nTo move beyond a flat \\\\\\\"flat\\\\\\\" look, use **Glassmorphism** for floating navigation bars or player controls.\\\\n*   **The Recipe:** Use &#96;surface_container&#96; at 70% opacity with a &#96;backdrop-filter: blur(20px)&#96;.\\\\n*   **Signature Gradients:** For primary CTAs, use a subtle linear gradient (Top-Left to Bottom-Right) from &#96;primary&#96; (#FFB3AE) to &#96;primary_container&#96; (#FF5351) to add a \\\\\\\"pulse\\\\\\\" of energy to the Coral-Red accent.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### 3. Typography\\\\nThe system uses a sophisticated pairing of **Manrope** (Display/Headlines) and **Inter** (Body/Labels).\\\\n\\\\n*   **Display (Manrope):** These are our \\\\\\\"Editorial Statements.\\\\\\\" Use &#96;display-lg&#96; (3.5rem) for movie titles in hero sections. Use negative letter-spacing (-0.02em) to create a high-fashion, dense look.\\\\n*   **Headline \u0026 Title (Manrope/Inter):** &#96;headline-md&#96; (1.75rem) serves as the primary gateway into a category. It should feel authoritative.\\\\n*   **Body \u0026 Label (Inter):** &#96;body-md&#96; (0.875rem) is used for synopses and metadata. Inter’s high x-height ensures legibility against the dark &#96;surface&#96; backdrop.\\\\n*   **The Tonal Hierarchy:** Primary information uses &#96;on_surface&#96; (#E5E2E1). Secondary metadata (Year, Rating, Genre) must use &#96;on_surface_variant&#96; (#E4BDBA) to reduce visual noise.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### 4. Elevation \u0026 Depth\\\\nWe eschew traditional drop shadows for **Tonal Layering**.\\\\n\\\\n*   **The Layering Principle:** Place a &#96;surface_container_lowest&#96; (#0E0E0E) card inside a &#96;surface_container_high&#96; (#2A2A2A) section. This \\\\\\\"recessed\\\\\\\" look creates an elegant, carved-out feel without the need for a single pixel of stroke.\\\\n*   **Ambient Shadows:** If an element must float (e.g., a Context Menu), use a shadow with a 40px blur and 6% opacity, using the &#96;surface_container_lowest&#96; color as the shadow tint. This mimics natural light dissipation.\\\\n*   **The Ghost Border:** If a boundary is required for accessibility, use the &#96;outline_variant&#96; token at **15% opacity**. This provides a \\\\\\\"hint\\\\\\\" of a container without breaking the minimal aesthetic.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### 5. Components\\\\n\\\\n#### Buttons\\\\n*   **Primary:** Background: &#96;primary&#96; gradient. Shape: &#96;0.5rem&#96; (8px). Typography: &#96;title-sm&#96; (Inter Semi-Bold).\\\\n*   **Secondary:** Background: &#96;surface_container_highest&#96; (#353534). For a premium feel, add a 10% opacity &#96;primary&#96; tint to the background.\\\\n*   **Ghost/Tertiary:** No background. Use &#96;on_surface&#96; text with a &#96;primary&#96; icon.\\\\n\\\\n#### Content Cards (The Core Unit)\\\\n*   **Geometry:** Always use &#96;lg&#96; (1rem/16px) for the outer container and &#96;md&#96; (0.75rem/12px) for internal elements (like \\\\\\\"New\\\\\\\" badges).\\\\n*   **Nesting:** Place &#96;on_surface&#96; typography directly onto the image using a &#96;surface_container_lowest&#96; gradient overlay at the bottom 30% of the card.\\\\n*   **Spacing:** Content within cards uses &#96;3&#96; (1rem) padding.\\\\n\\\\n#### Chips \u0026 Badges\\\\n*   **Selection Chips:** Use &#96;secondary_container&#96; (#822625) for the active state to provide a moody, deep-red glow that isn't as aggressive as the primary CTA.\\\\n\\\\n#### Lists \u0026 Carousels\\\\n*   **Strict Rule:** **Forbid dividers.** Use &#96;6&#96; (2rem) of horizontal whitespace between carousel items. If list items require separation, use a subtle background shift to &#96;surface_container_low&#96; on hover.\\\\n\\\\n#### Navigation Rail (Unique OTT Component)\\\\n*   Instead of a top bar, use a side-aligned Navigation Rail. Use **Glassmorphism** (70% opacity + blur) to allow movie poster art to bleed through as the user scrolls, keeping the experience immersive.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### 6. Do’s and Don'ts\\\\n\\\\n*   **DO** use &#96;surface_bright&#96; (#3A3939) for hover states on dark cards to create a \\\\\\\"lit from within\\\\\\\" effect.\\\\n*   **DO** use intentional asymmetry—allow a \\\\\\\"Featured\\\\\\\" movie poster to take up 65% of the screen width while metadata takes up 35%.\\\\n*   **DON'T** use 100% white (#FFFFFF) for long-form text; use &#96;on_surface&#96; (#E5E2E1) to reduce eye strain in dark environments.\\\\n*   **DON'T** ever use a solid &#96;outline&#96; at 100% opacity. It breaks the \\\\\\\"Cinematic\\\\\\\" immersion and looks like a generic web app.\\\\n*   **DO** prioritize \\\\\\\"The Breath.\\\\\\\" If a screen feels crowded, double the spacing token (e.g., move from &#96;5&#96; to &#96;10&#96;). \\\"hidden\\\":true},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"a07c5eaded494389bd43092e0a4a9ec7\\\",\\\"sourceScreen\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476/screens/a07c5eaded494389bd43092e0a4a9ec7\\\",\\\"x\\\":2976,\\\"y\\\":8,\\\"width\\\":390,\\\"height\\\":1492},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"assets-edd57418919f4c5e80996eaf341072c3-1774849626547\\\",\\\"width\\\":960,\\\"height\\\":540,\\\"sourceAsset\\\":\\\"assets/edd57418919f4c5e80996eaf341072c3\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"DESIGN_SYSTEM_INSTANCE\\\"},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"dedc0208bb5243f99a9097796e19acb1\\\",\\\"sourceScreen\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476/screens/dedc0208bb5243f99a9097796e19acb1\\\",\\\"x\\\":1582,\\\"y\\\":10,\\\"width\\\":390,\\\"height\\\":1117},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"dfd496e9071f42ed84136f1fe141516a\\\",\\\"sourceScreen\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476/screens/dfd496e9071f42ed84136f1fe141516a\\\",\\\"x\\\":1120,\\\"y\\\":2394,\\\"width\\\":390,\\\"height\\\":2030,\\\"hidden\\\":true},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"f0dfc9d6fb784fbaa6648270b6cabc00\\\",\\\"sourceScreen\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476/screens/f0dfc9d6fb784fbaa6648270b6cabc00\\\",\\\"x\\\":5027,\\\"y\\\":48,\\\"width\\\":390,\\\"height\\\":884,\\\"hidden\\\":true},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"f4ab24a965cb474b838fc201d3dbe5bb\\\",\\\"sourceScreen\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476/screens/f4ab24a965cb474b838fc201d3dbe5bb\\\",\\\"x\\\":2545,\\\"y\\\":2440,\\\"width\\\":390,\\\"height\\\":1228,\\\"hidden\\\":true},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"f8df68ac4f5b4dbaa5bfa92c7d62f756\\\",\\\"sourceScreen\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476/screens/f8df68ac4f5b4dbaa5bfa92c7d62f756\\\",\\\"x\\\":1120,\\\"width\\\":390,\\\"height\\\":2152}],\\\"metadata\\\":{\\\"userRole\\\":\\\"READER\\\"},\\\"readTime\\\":\\\"2026-04-23T11:18:31.717922Z\\\"}]}\"}],\"structuredContent\":{\"projects\":[{\"designTheme\":{\"bodyFont\":\"INTER\",\"colorMode\":\"DARK\",\"colorVariant\":\"FIDELITY\",\"customColor\":\"#E5383B\",\"designMd\":\"# Design System Strategy: The Cinematic Curator\\n\\n## 1. Overview \u0026 Creative North Star\\nThe North Star for this design system is **\\\"The Cinematic Curator.\\\"** \\n\\nIn a world of cluttered OTT interfaces, this system rejects the \\\"infinite grid\\\" in favor of a high-end editorial experience. We treat digital real estate like a luxury film program. To move beyond a \\\"template\\\" look, we leverage **intentional asymmetry**—such as oversized hero titles overlapping content cards—and a rigorous **tonal layering** strategy. This creates a sense of \\\"physical\\\" depth where content isn't just displayed; it is presented. The interface should feel like a darkened theater where only the most important elements catch the light.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 2. Colors \u0026 Surface Architecture\\nOur palette is rooted in the \\\"Absolute Dark\\\" philosophy. We do not use pure black (#000) for surfaces to avoid \\\"crushing\\\" the visual depth. Instead, we use deep charcoals and warm bordeaux-tinted grays.\\n\\n#### The \\\"No-Line\\\" Rule\\n**Explicit Instruction:** Designers are prohibited from using 1px solid borders to define sections or containers. \\nBoundaries must be defined solely through:\\n1.  **Background Shifts:** Moving from &#96;surface&#96; (#131313) to &#96;surface_container_low&#96; (#1C1B1B).\\n2.  **Vertical Breathing Room:** Utilizing the &#96;8&#96; (2.75rem) or &#96;10&#96; (3.5rem) spacing tokens to signal a change in context.\\n\\n#### Surface Hierarchy \u0026 Nesting\\nTreat the UI as a series of stacked layers. The closer an element is to the user, the \\\"brighter\\\" its surface becomes:\\n*   **Base Layer:** &#96;surface&#96; (#131313) for the main application background.\\n*   **Sectional Layer:** &#96;surface_container_low&#96; (#1C1B1B) for large content groupings.\\n*   **Interactive Layer:** &#96;surface_container_highest&#96; (#353534) for cards, modals, or hovering states.\\n\\n#### Glass \u0026 Gradient Implementation\\nTo move beyond a flat \\\"flat\\\" look, use **Glassmorphism** for floating navigation bars or player controls.\\n*   **The Recipe:** Use &#96;surface_container&#96; at 70% opacity with a &#96;backdrop-filter: blur(20px)&#96;.\\n*   **Signature Gradients:** For primary CTAs, use a subtle linear gradient (Top-Left to Bottom-Right) from &#96;primary&#96; (#FFB3AE) to &#96;primary_container&#96; (#FF5351) to add a \\\"pulse\\\" of energy to the Coral-Red accent.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 3. Typography\\nThe system uses a sophisticated pairing of **Manrope** (Display/Headlines) and **Inter** (Body/Labels).\\n\\n*   **Display (Manrope):** These are our \\\"Editorial Statements.\\\" Use &#96;display-lg&#96; (3.5rem) for movie titles in hero sections. Use negative letter-spacing (-0.02em) to create a high-fashion, dense look.\\n*   **Headline \u0026 Title (Manrope/Inter):** &#96;headline-md&#96; (1.75rem) serves as the primary gateway into a category. It should feel authoritative.\\n*   **Body \u0026 Label (Inter):** &#96;body-md&#96; (0.875rem) is used for synopses and metadata. Inter’s high x-height ensures legibility against the dark &#96;surface&#96; backdrop.\\n*   **The Tonal Hierarchy:** Primary information uses &#96;on_surface&#96; (#E5E2E1). Secondary metadata (Year, Rating, Genre) must use &#96;on_surface_variant&#96; (#E4BDBA) to reduce visual noise.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 4. Elevation \u0026 Depth\\nWe eschew traditional drop shadows for **Tonal Layering**.\\n\\n*   **The Layering Principle:** Place a &#96;surface_container_lowest&#96; (#0E0E0E) card inside a &#96;surface_container_high&#96; (#2A2A2A) section. This \\\"recessed\\\" look creates an elegant, carved-out feel without the need for a single pixel of stroke.\\n*   **Ambient Shadows:** If an element must float (e.g., a Context Menu), use a shadow with a 40px blur and 6% opacity, using the &#96;surface_container_lowest&#96; color as the shadow tint. This mimics natural light dissipation.\\n*   **The Ghost Border:** If a boundary is required for accessibility, use the &#96;outline_variant&#96; token at **15% opacity**. This provides a \\\"hint\\\" of a container without breaking the minimal aesthetic.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 5. Components\\n\\n#### Buttons\\n*   **Primary:** Background: &#96;primary&#96; gradient. Shape: &#96;0.5rem&#96; (8px). Typography: &#96;title-sm&#96; (Inter Semi-Bold).\\n*   **Secondary:** Background: &#96;surface_container_highest&#96; (#353534). For a premium feel, add a 10% opacity &#96;primary&#96; tint to the background.\\n*   **Ghost/Tertiary:** No background. Use &#96;on_surface&#96; text with a &#96;primary&#96; icon.\\n\\n#### Content Cards (The Core Unit)\\n*   **Geometry:** Always use &#96;lg&#96; (1rem/16px) for the outer container and &#96;md&#96; (0.75rem/12px) for internal elements (like \\\"New\\\" badges).\\n*   **Nesting:** Place &#96;on_surface&#96; typography directly onto the image using a &#96;surface_container_lowest&#96; gradient overlay at the bottom 30% of the card.\\n*   **Spacing:** Content within cards uses &#96;3&#96; (1rem) padding.\\n\\n#### Chips \u0026 Badges\\n*   **Selection Chips:** Use &#96;secondary_container&#96; (#822625) for the active state to provide a moody, deep-red glow that isn't as aggressive as the primary CTA.\\n\\n#### Lists \u0026 Carousels\\n*   **Strict Rule:** **Forbid dividers.** Use &#96;6&#96; (2rem) of horizontal whitespace between carousel items. If list items require separation, use a subtle background shift to &#96;surface_container_low&#96; on hover.\\n\\n#### Navigation Rail (Unique OTT Component)\\n*   Instead of a top bar, use a side-aligned Navigation Rail. Use **Glassmorphism** (70% opacity + blur) to allow movie poster art to bleed through as the user scrolls, keeping the experience immersive.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 6. Do’s and Don'ts\\n\\n*   **DO** use &#96;surface_bright&#96; (#3A3939) for hover states on dark cards to create a \\\"lit from within\\\" effect.\\n*   **DO** use intentional asymmetry—allow a \\\"Featured\\\" movie poster to take up 65% of the screen width while metadata takes up 35%.\\n*   **DON'T** use 100% white (#FFFFFF) for long-form text; use &#96;on_surface&#96; (#E5E2E1) to reduce eye strain in dark environments.\\n*   **DON'T** ever use a solid &#96;outline&#96; at 100% opacity. It breaks the \\\"Cinematic\\\" immersion and looks like a generic web app.\\n*   **DO** prioritize \\\"The Breath.\\\" If a screen feels crowded, double the spacing token (e.g., move from &#96;5&#96; to &#96;10&#96;). High-end design is defined by what you leave out.\",\"font\":\"MANROPE\",\"headlineFont\":\"MANROPE\",\"labelFont\":\"INTER\",\"namedColors\":{\"background\":\"#131313\",\"error\":\"#ffb4ab\",\"error_container\":\"#93000a\",\"inverse_on_surface\":\"#313030\",\"inverse_primary\":\"#bb1522\",\"inverse_surface\":\"#e5e2e1\",\"on_background\":\"#e5e2e1\",\"on_error\":\"#690005\",\"on_error_container\":\"#ffdad6\",\"on_primary\":\"#68000b\",\"on_primary_container\":\"#5c0008\",\"on_primary_fixed\":\"#410004\",\"on_primary_fixed_variant\":\"#930014\",\"on_secondary\":\"#630e11\",\"on_secondary_container\":\"#ff9992\",\"on_secondary_fixed\":\"#410004\",\"on_secondary_fixed_variant\":\"#822625\",\"on_surface\":\"#e5e2e1\",\"on_surface_variant\":\"#e4bdba\",\"on_tertiary\":\"#00363b\",\"on_tertiary_container\":\"#002f33\",\"on_tertiary_fixed\":\"#002023\",\"on_tertiary_fixed_variant\":\"#004f55\",\"outline\":\"#ab8886\",\"outline_variant\":\"#5b403e\",\"primary\":\"#ffb3ae\",\"primary_container\":\"#ff5351\",\"primary_fixed\":\"#ffdad7\",\"primary_fixed_dim\":\"#ffb3ae\",\"secondary\":\"#ffb3ae\",\"secondary_container\":\"#822625\",\"secondary_fixed\":\"#ffdad7\",\"secondary_fixed_dim\":\"#ffb3ae\",\"surface\":\"#131313\",\"surface_bright\":\"#3a3939\",\"surface_container\":\"#201f1f\",\"surface_container_high\":\"#2a2a2a\",\"surface_container_highest\":\"#353534\",\"surface_container_low\":\"#1c1b1b\",\"surface_container_lowest\":\"#0e0e0e\",\"surface_dim\":\"#131313\",\"surface_tint\":\"#ffb3ae\",\"surface_variant\":\"#353534\",\"tertiary\":\"#71d5e0\",\"tertiary_container\":\"#319ea9\",\"tertiary_fixed\":\"#8ef2fd\",\"tertiary_fixed_dim\":\"#71d5e0\"},\"overrideNeutralColor\":\"#0F0F0F\",\"overridePrimaryColor\":\"#E5383B\",\"roundness\":\"ROUND_EIGHT\",\"spacingScale\":3},\"deviceType\":\"MOBILE\",\"metadata\":{\"userRole\":\"READER\"},\"name\":\"projects/18386357471434296476\",\"origin\":\"STITCH\",\"projectType\":\"TEXT_TO_UI_PRO\",\"readTime\":\"2026-04-23T11:18:31.717922Z\",\"screenInstances\":[{\"height\":1302 "{\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"{\\\"name\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Search\\\",\\\"visibility\\\":\\\"PUBLIC\\\",\\\"createTime\\\":\\\"2026-03-30T05:46:29.517940Z\\\",\\\"updateTime\\\":\\\"2026-03-30T06:21:21.468340Z\\\",\\\"projectType\\\":\\\"TEXT_TO_UI_PRO\\\",\\\"thumbnailScreenshot\\\":{\\\"name\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476/files/21f48134060543a5bd8944a3aee7f166\\\",\\\"downloadUrl\\\":\\\"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/aida/ADBb0ujKFTCaPVFSbEulUQgYVY7pYN2yw8XF9MZaRsfTOgf9E_b8iHV9lvYhB-zv9BOpU1K0EqEFv_8WGeCY_W-vRIkZzxm4s2tNWq6gKKhatELZVOwQ_a91WKqNZRWzVbqrB5Z5F1f8rwBZ_evWvD5wDgncY5Tg0NFvN8tNSkdQH6kp97f8AqM3eFx1WuuEpdCjA4LVGMIbQ_rv8U_dMetx_RHZrCjVicN7_WrL0296FYJlIr6znuM60ksWOqdy\\\"},\\\"origin\\\":\\\"STITCH\\\",\\\"deviceType\\\":\\\"MOBILE\\\",\\\"designTheme\\\":{\\\"colorMode\\\":\\\"DARK\\\",\\\"font\\\":\\\"MANROPE\\\",\\\"roundness\\\":\\\"ROUND_EIGHT\\\",\\\"customColor\\\":\\\"#E5383B\\\",\\\"headlineFont\\\":\\\"MANROPE\\\",\\\"bodyFont\\\":\\\"INTER\\\",\\\"labelFont\\\":\\\"INTER\\\",\\\"namedColors\\\":{\\\"background\\\":\\\"#131313\\\",\\\"error\\\":\\\"#ffb4ab\\\",\\\"error_container\\\":\\\"#93000a\\\",\\\"inverse_on_surface\\\":\\\"#313030\\\",\\\"inverse_primary\\\":\\\"#bb1522\\\",\\\"inverse_surface\\\":\\\"#e5e2e1\\\",\\\"on_background\\\":\\\"#e5e2e1\\\",\\\"on_error\\\":\\\"#690005\\\",\\\"on_error_container\\\":\\\"#ffdad6\\\",\\\"on_primary\\\":\\\"#68000b\\\",\\\"on_primary_container\\\":\\\"#5c0008\\\",\\\"on_primary_fixed\\\":\\\"#410004\\\",\\\"on_primary_fixed_variant\\\":\\\"#930014\\\",\\\"on_secondary\\\":\\\"#630e11\\\",\\\"on_secondary_container\\\":\\\"#ff9992\\\",\\\"on_secondary_fixed\\\":\\\"#410004\\\",\\\"on_secondary_fixed_variant\\\":\\\"#822625\\\",\\\"on_surface\\\":\\\"#e5e2e1\\\",\\\"on_surface_variant\\\":\\\"#e4bdba\\\",\\\"on_tertiary\\\":\\\"#00363b\\\",\\\"on_tertiary_container\\\":\\\"#002f33\\\",\\\"on_tertiary_fixed\\\":\\\"#002023\\\",\\\"on_tertiary_fixed_variant\\\":\\\"#004f55\\\",\\\"outline\\\":\\\"#ab8886\\\",\\\"outline_variant\\\":\\\"#5b403e\\\",\\\"primary\\\":\\\"#ffb3ae\\\",\\\"primary_container\\\":\\\"#ff5351\\\",\\\"primary_fixed\\\":\\\"#ffdad7\\\",\\\"primary_fixed_dim\\\":\\\"#ffb3ae\\\",\\\"secondary\\\":\\\"#ffb3ae\\\",\\\"secondary_container\\\":\\\"#822625\\\",\\\"secondary_fixed\\\":\\\"#ffdad7\\\",\\\"secondary_fixed_dim\\\":\\\"#ffb3ae\\\",\\\"surface\\\":\\\"#131313\\\",\\\"surface_bright\\\":\\\"#3a3939\\\",\\\"surface_container\\\":\\\"#201f1f\\\",\\\"surface_container_high\\\":\\\"#2a2a2a\\\",\\\"surface_container_highest\\\":\\\"#353534\\\",\\\"surface_container_low\\\":\\\"#1c1b1b\\\",\\\"surface_container_lowest\\\":\\\"#0e0e0e\\\",\\\"surface_dim\\\":\\\"#131313\\\",\\\"surface_tint\\\":\\\"#ffb3ae\\\",\\\"surface_variant\\\":\\\"#353534\\\",\\\"tertiary\\\":\\\"#71d5e0\\\",\\\"tertiary_container\\\":\\\"#319ea9\\\",\\\"tertiary_fixed\\\":\\\"#8ef2fd\\\",\\\"tertiary_fixed_dim\\\":\\\"#71d5e0\\\"},\\\"designMd\\\":\\\"# Design System Strategy: The Cinematic Curator\\\\n\\\\n## 1. Overview \u0026 Creative North Star\\\\nThe North Star for this design system is **\\\\\\\"The Cinematic Curator.\\\\\\\"** \\\\n\\\\nIn a world of cluttered OTT interfaces, this system rejects the \\\\\\\"infinite grid\\\\\\\" in favor of a high-end editorial experience. We treat digital real estate like a luxury film program. To move beyond a \\\\\\\"template\\\\\\\" look, we leverage **intentional asymmetry**—such as oversized hero titles overlapping content cards—and a rigorous **tonal layering** strategy. This creates a sense of \\\\\\\"physical\\\\\\\" depth where content isn't just displayed; it is presented. The interface should feel like a darkened theater where only the most important elements catch the light.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### 2. Colors \u0026 Surface Architecture\\\\nOur palette is rooted in the \\\\\\\"Absolute Dark\\\\\\\" philosophy. We do not use pure black (#000) for surfaces to avoid \\\\\\\"crushing\\\\\\\" the visual depth. Instead, we use deep charcoals and warm bordeaux-tinted grays.\\\\n\\\\n#### The \\\\\\\"No-Line\\\\\\\" Rule\\\\n**Explicit Instruction:** Designers are prohibited from using 1px solid borders to define sections or containers. \\\\nBoundaries must be defined solely through:\\\\n1.  **Background Shifts:** Moving from &#96;surface&#96; (#131313) to &#96;surface_container_low&#96; (#1C1B1B).\\\\n2.  **Vertical Breathing Room:** Utilizing the &#96;8&#96; (2.75rem) or &#96;10&#96; (3.5rem) spacing tokens to signal a change in context.\\\\n\\\\n#### Surface Hierarchy \u0026 Nesting\\\\nTreat the UI as a series of stacked layers. The closer an element is to the user, the \\\\\\\"brighter\\\\\\\" its surface becomes:\\\\n*   **Base Layer:** &#96;surface&#96; (#131313) for the main application background.\\\\n*   **Sectional Layer:** &#96;surface_container_low&#96; (#1C1B1B) for large content groupings.\\\\n*   **Interactive Layer:** &#96;surface_container_highest&#96; (#353534) for cards, modals, or hovering states.\\\\n\\\\n#### Glass \u0026 Gradient Implementation\\\\nTo move beyond a flat \\\\\\\"flat\\\\\\\" look, use **Glassmorphism** for floating navigation bars or player controls.\\\\n*   **The Recipe:** Use &#96;surface_container&#96; at 70% opacity with a &#96;backdrop-filter: blur(20px)&#96;.\\\\n*   **Signature Gradients:** For primary CTAs, use a subtle linear gradient (Top-Left to Bottom-Right) from &#96;primary&#96; (#FFB3AE) to &#96;primary_container&#96; (#FF5351) to add a \\\\\\\"pulse\\\\\\\" of energy to the Coral-Red accent.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### 3. Typography\\\\nThe system uses a sophisticated pairing of **Manrope** (Display/Headlines) and **Inter** (Body/Labels).\\\\n\\\\n*   **Display (Manrope):** These are our \\\\\\\"Editorial Statements.\\\\\\\" Use &#96;display-lg&#96; (3.5rem) for movie titles in hero sections. Use negative letter-spacing (-0.02em) to create a high-fashion, dense look.\\\\n*   **Headline \u0026 Title (Manrope/Inter):** &#96;headline-md&#96; (1.75rem) serves as the primary gateway into a category. It should feel authoritative.\\\\n*   **Body \u0026 Label (Inter):** &#96;body-md&#96; (0.875rem) is used for synopses and metadata. Inter’s high x-height ensures legibility against the dark &#96;surface&#96; backdrop.\\\\n*   **The Tonal Hierarchy:** Primary information uses &#96;on_surface&#96; (#E5E2E1). Secondary metadata (Year, Rating, Genre) must use &#96;on_surface_variant&#96; (#E4BDBA) to reduce visual noise.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### 4. Elevation \u0026 Depth\\\\nWe eschew traditional drop shadows for **Tonal Layering**.\\\\n\\\\n*   **The Layering Principle:** Place a &#96;surface_container_lowest&#96; (#0E0E0E) card inside a &#96;surface_container_high&#96; (#2A2A2A) section. This \\\\\\\"recessed\\\\\\\" look creates an elegant, carved-out feel without the need for a single pixel of stroke.\\\\n*   **Ambient Shadows:** If an element must float (e.g., a Context Menu), use a shadow with a 40px blur and 6% opacity, using the &#96;surface_container_lowest&#96; color as the shadow tint. This mimics natural light dissipation.\\\\n*   **The Ghost Border:** If a boundary is required for accessibility, use the &#96;outline_variant&#96; token at **15% opacity**. This provides a \\\\\\\"hint\\\\\\\" of a container without breaking the minimal aesthetic.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### 5. Components\\\\n\\\\n#### Buttons\\\\n*   **Primary:** Background: &#96;primary&#96; gradient. Shape: &#96;0.5rem&#96; (8px). Typography: &#96;title-sm&#96; (Inter Semi-Bold).\\\\n*   **Secondary:** Background: &#96;surface_container_highest&#96; (#353534). For a premium feel, add a 10% opacity &#96;primary&#96; tint to the background.\\\\n*   **Ghost/Tertiary:** No background. Use &#96;on_surface&#96; text with a &#96;primary&#96; icon.\\\\n\\\\n#### Content Cards (The Core Unit)\\\\n*   **Geometry:** Always use &#96;lg&#96; (1rem/16px) for the outer container and &#96;md&#96; (0.75rem/12px) for internal elements (like \\\\\\\"New\\\\\\\" badges).\\\\n*   **Nesting:** Place &#96;on_surface&#96; typography directly onto the image using a &#96;surface_container_lowest&#96; gradient overlay at the bottom 30% of the card.\\\\n*   **Spacing:** Content within cards uses &#96;3&#96; (1rem) padding.\\\\n\\\\n#### Chips \u0026 Badges\\\\n*   **Selection Chips:** Use &#96;secondary_container&#96; (#822625) for the active state to provide a moody, deep-red glow that isn't as aggressive as the primary CTA.\\\\n\\\\n#### Lists \u0026 Carousels\\\\n*   **Strict Rule:** **Forbid dividers.** Use &#96;6&#96; (2rem) of horizontal whitespace between carousel items. If list items require separation, use a subtle background shift to &#96;surface_container_low&#96; on hover.\\\\n\\\\n#### Navigation Rail (Unique OTT Component)\\\\n*   Instead of a top bar, use a side-aligned Navigation Rail. Use **Glassmorphism** (70% opacity + blur) to allow movie poster art to bleed through as the user scrolls, keeping the experience immersive.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### 6. Do’s and Don'ts\\\\n\\\\n*   **DO** use &#96;surface_bright&#96; (#3A3939) for hover states on dark cards to create a \\\\\\\"lit from within\\\\\\\" effect.\\\\n*   **DO** use intentional asymmetry—allow a \\\\\\\"Featured\\\\\\\" movie poster to take up 65% of the screen width while metadata takes up 35%.\\\\n*   **DON'T** use 100% white (#FFFFFF) for long-form text; use &#96;on_surface&#96; (#E5E2E1) to reduce eye strain in dark environments.\\\\n*   **DON'T** ever use a solid &#96;outline&#96; at 100% opacity. It breaks the \\\\\\\"Cinematic\\\\\\\" immersion and looks like a generic web app.\\\\n*   **DO** prioritize \\\\\\\"The Breath.\\\\\\\" If a screen feels crowded, double the spacing token (e.g., move from &#96;5&#96; to &#96;10&#96;). High-end design is defined by what you leave out.\\\",\\\"colorVariant\\\":\\\"FIDELITY\\\",\\\"overridePrimaryColor\\\":\\\"#E5383B\\\",\\\"overrideNeutralColor\\\":\\\"#0F0F0F\\\",\\\"spacingScale\\\":3},\\\"screenInstances\\\":[{\\\"id\\\":\\\"051e00c95b48445a8f09b36be4a0cf01\\\",\\\"sourceScreen\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476/screens/051e00c95b48445a8f09b36be4a0cf01\\\",\\\"x\\\":2465,\\\"y\\\":10,\\\"width\\\":390,\\\"height\\\":1302},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"0622da3a85f04ec5ab26c7a156b5f38d\\\",\\\"sourceScreen\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476/screens/0622da3a85f04ec5ab26c7a156b5f38d\\\",\\\"x\\\":1478,\\\"width\\\":390,\\\"height\\\":2152,\\\"hidden\\\":true},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"14dc681cf67d4ac99b836bb1977ee992\\\",\\\"sourceScreen\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476/screens/14dc681cf67d4ac99b836bb1977ee992\\\",\\\"x\\\":2002,\\\"y\\\":8,\\\"width\\\":390,\\\"height\\\":1525},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"531dbe2167a049c3ab0cb003bb697909\\\",\\\"sourceScreen\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476/screens/531dbe2167a049c3ab0cb003bb697909\\\",\\\"x\\\":2075,\\\"y\\\":2447,\\\"width\\\":390,\\\"height\\\":884,\\\"hidden\\\":true},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"71cd040f2d2947c79e5abbc1a2534b6f\\\",\\\"sourceScreen\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476/screens/71cd040f2d2947c79e5abbc1a2534b6f\\\",\\\"x\\\":1622,\\\"y\\\":2409,\\\"width\\\":390,\\\"height\\\":1302,\\\"hidden\\\":true},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"7219607cf63041f18d10a5294e572910\\\",\\\"sourceScreen\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476/screens/7219607cf63041f18d10a5294e572910\\\",\\\"x\\\":4079,\\\"y\\\":51,\\\"width\\\":390,\\\"height\\\":1076,\\\"hidden\\\":true},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"8c9dd9c64d66497dab5106dc5ed4be05\\\",\\\"sourceScreen\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476/screens/8c9dd9c64d66497dab5106dc5ed4be05\\\",\\\"x\\\":3426,\\\"y\\\":19,\\\"width\\\":390,\\\"height\\\":1096},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"98752323786c449585eeb94cf5b80b3b\\\",\\\"sourceScreen\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476/screens/98752323786c449585eeb94cf5b80b3b\\\",\\\"x\\\":6082,\\\"width\\\":390,\\\"height\\\":884,\\\"hidden\\\":true},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"a07c5eaded494389bd43092e0a4a9ec7\\\",\\\"sourceScreen\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476/screens/a07c5eaded494389bd43092e0a4a9ec7\\\",\\\"x\\\":2976,\\\"y\\\":8,\\\"width\\\":390,\\\"height\\\":1492},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"assets-edd57418919f4c5e80996eaf341072c3-1774849626547\\\",\\\"width\\\":960,\\\"height\\\":540,\\\"sourceAsset\\\":\\\"assets/edd57418919f4c5e80996eaf341072c3\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"DESIGN_SYSTEM_INSTANCE\\\"},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"dedc0208bb5243f99a9097796e19acb1\\\",\\\"sourceScreen\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476/screens/dedc0208bb5243f99a9097796e19acb1\\\",\\\"x\\\":1582,\\\"y\\\":10,\\\"width\\\":390,\\\"height\\\":1117},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"dfd496e9071f42ed84136f1fe141516a\\\",\\\"sourceScreen\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476/screens/dfd496e9071f42ed84136f1fe141516a\\\",\\\"x\\\":1120,\\\"y\\\":2394,\\\"width\\\":390,\\\"height\\\":2030,\\\"hidden\\\":true},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"f0dfc9d6fb784fbaa6648270b6cabc00\\\",\\\"sourceScreen\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476/screens/f0dfc9d6fb784fbaa6648270b6cabc00\\\",\\\"x\\\":5027,\\\"y\\\":48,\\\"width\\\":390,\\\"height\\\":884,\\\"hidden\\\":true},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"f4ab24a965cb474b838fc201d3dbe5bb\\\",\\\"sourceScreen\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476/screens/f4ab24a965cb474b838fc201d3dbe5bb\\\",\\\"x\\\":2545,\\\"y\\\":2440,\\\"width\\\":390,\\\"height\\\":1228,\\\"hidden\\\":true},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"f8df68ac4f5b4dbaa5bfa92c7d62f756\\\",\\\"sourceScreen\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476/screens/f8df68ac4f5b4dbaa5bfa92c7d62f756\\\",\\\"x\\\":1120,\\\"width\\\":390,\\\"height\\\":2152}],\\\"metadata\\\":{\\\"userRole\\\":\\\"READER\\\"}}\"}],\"structuredContent\":{\"createTime\":\"2026-03-30T05:46:29.517940Z\",\"designTheme\":{\"bodyFont\":\"INTER\",\"colorMode\":\"DARK\",\"colorVariant\":\"FIDELITY\",\"customColor\":\"#E5383B\",\"designMd\":\"# Design System Strategy: The Cinematic Curator\\n\\n## 1. Overview \u0026 Creative North Star\\nThe North Star for this design system is **\\\"The Cinematic Curator.\\\"** \\n\\nIn a world of cluttered OTT interfaces, this system rejects the \\\"infinite grid\\\" in favor of a high-end editorial experience. We treat digital real estate like a luxury film program. To move beyond a \\\"template\\\" look, we leverage **intentional asymmetry**—such as oversized hero titles overlapping content cards—and a rigorous **tonal layering** strategy. This creates a sense of \\\"physical\\\" depth where content isn't just displayed; it is presented. The interface should feel like a darkened theater where only the most important elements catch the light.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 2. Colors \u0026 Surface Architecture\\nOur palette is rooted in the \\\"Absolute Dark\\\" philosophy. We do not use pure black (#000) for surfaces to avoid \\\"crushing\\\" the visual depth. Instead, we use deep charcoals and warm bordeaux-tinted grays.\\n\\n#### The \\\"No-Line\\\" Rule\\n**Explicit Instruction:** Designers are prohibited from using 1px solid borders to define sections or containers. \\nBoundaries must be defined solely through:\\n1.  **Background Shifts:** Moving from &#96;surface&#96; (#131313) to &#96;surface_container_low&#96; (#1C1B1B).\\n2.  **Vertical Breathing Room:** Utilizing the &#96;8&#96; (2.75rem) or &#96;10&#96; (3.5rem) spacing tokens to signal a change in context.\\n\\n#### Surface Hierarchy \u0026 Nesting\\nTreat the UI as a series of stacked layers. The closer an element is to the user, the \\\"brighter\\\" its surface becomes:\\n*   **Base Layer:** &#96;surface&#96; (#131313) for the main application background.\\n*   **Sectional Layer:** &#96;surface_container_low&#96; (#1C1B1B) for large content groupings.\\n*   **Interactive Layer:** &#96;surface_container_highest&#96; (#353534) for cards, modals, or hovering states.\\n\\n#### Glass \u0026 Gradient Implementation\\nTo move beyond a flat \\\"flat\\\" look, use **Glassmorphism** for floating navigation bars or player controls.\\n*   **The Recipe:** Use &#96;surface_container&#96; at 70% opacity with a &#96;backdrop-filter: blur(20px)&#96;.\\n*   **Signature Gradients:** For primary CTAs, use a subtle linear gradient (Top-Left to Bottom-Right) from &#96;primary&#96; (#FFB3AE) to &#96;primary_container&#96; (#FF5351) to add a \\\"pulse\\\" of energy to the Coral-Red accent.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 3. Typography\\nThe system uses a sophisticated pairing of **Manrope** (Display/Headlines) and **Inter** (Body/Labels).\\n\\n*   **Display (Manrope):** These are our \\\"Editorial Statements.\\\" Use &#96;display-lg&#96; (3.5rem) for movie titles in hero sections. Use negative letter-spacing (-0.02em) to create a high-fashion, dense look.\\n*   **Headline \u0026 Title (Manrope/Inter):** &#96;headline-md&#96; (1.75rem) serves as the primary gateway into a category. It should feel authoritative.\\n*   **Body \u0026 Label (Inter):** &#96;body-md&#96; (0.875rem) is used for synopses and metadata. Inter’s high x-height ensures legibility against the dark &#96;surface&#96; backdrop.\\n*   **The Tonal Hierarchy:** Primary information uses &#96;on_surface&#96; (#E5E2E1). Secondary metadata (Year, Rating, Genre) must use &#96;on_surface_variant&#96; (#E4BDBA) to reduce visual noise.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 4. Elevation \u0026 Depth\\nWe eschew traditional drop shadows for **Tonal Layering**.\\n\\n*   **The Layering Principle:** Place a &#96;surface_container_lowest&#96; (#0E0E0E) card inside a &#96;surface_container_high&#96; (#2A2A2A) section. This \\\"recessed\\\" look creates an elegant, carved-out feel without the need for a single pixel of stroke.\\n*   **Ambient Shadows:** If an element must float (e.g., a Context Menu), use a shadow with a 40px blur and 6% opacity, using the &#96;surface_container_lowest&#96; color as the shadow tint. This mimics natural light dissipation.\\n*   **The Ghost Border:** If a boundary is required for accessibility, use the &#96;outline_variant&#96; token at **15% opacity**. This provides a \\\"hint\\\" of a container without breaking the minimal aesthetic.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 5. Components\\n\\n#### Buttons\\n*   **Primary:** Background: &#96;primary&#96; gradient. Shape: &#96;0.5rem&#96; (8px). Typography: &#96;title-sm&#96; (Inter Semi-Bold).\\n*   **Secondary:** Background: &#96;surface_container_highest&#96; (#353534). For a premium feel, add a 10% opacity &#96;primary&#96; tint to the background.\\n*   **Ghost/Tertiary:** No background. Use &#96;on_surface&#96; text with a &#96;primary&#96; icon.\\n\\n#### Content Cards (The Core Unit)\\n*   **Geometry:** Always use &#96;lg&#96; (1rem/16px) for the outer container and &#96;md&#96; (0.75rem/12px) for internal elements (like \\\"New\\\" badges).\\n*   **Nesting:** Place &#96;on_surface&#96; typography directly onto the image using a &#96;surface_container_lowest&#96; gradient overlay at the bottom 30% of the card.\\n*   **Spacing:** Content within cards uses &#96;3&#96; (1rem) padding.\\n\\n#### Chips \u0026 Badges\\n*   **Selection Chips:** Use &#96;secondary_container&#96; (#822625) for the active state to provide a moody, deep-red glow that isn't as aggressive as the primary CTA.\\n\\n#### Lists \u0026 Carousels\\n*   **Strict Rule:** **Forbid dividers.** Use &#96;6&#96; (2rem) of horizontal whitespace between carousel items. If list items require separation, use a subtle background shift to &#96;surface_container_low&#96; on hover.\\n\\n#### Navigation Rail (Unique OTT Component)\\n*   Instead of a top bar, use a side-aligned Navigation Rail. Use **Glassmorphism** (70% opacity + blur) to allow movie poster art to bleed through as the user scrolls, keeping the experience immersive.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 6. Do’s and Don'ts\\n\\n*   **DO** use &#96;surface_bright&#96; (#3A3939) for hover states on dark cards to create a \\\"lit from within\\\" effect.\\n*   **DO** use intentional asymmetry—allow a \\\"Featured\\\" movie poster to take up 65% of the screen width while metadata takes up 35%.\\n*   **DON'T** use 100% white (#FFFFFF) for long-form text; use &#96;on_surface&#96; (#E5E2E1) to reduce eye strain in dark environments.\\n*   **DON'T** ever use a solid &#96;outline&#96; at 100% opacity. It breaks the \\\"Cinematic\\\" immersion and looks like a generic web app.\\n*   **DO** prioritize \\\"The Breath.\\\" If a screen feels crowded, double the spacing token (e.g., move from &#96;5&#96; to &#96;10&#96;). [New Google Stitch Just Change Design Forever](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EfnIdEPF8MU)\n\n# Google Stitch UI - Design with AI\n\n**What is Stitch?**\nStitch is an AI-native software design canvas from Google Labs that allows anyone to create, iterate, and collaborate on high-fidelity UI designs from natural language.[2] It transforms design and development workflows by turning simple text descriptions and images into functional UI designs and frontend code.[4]\n\n**Key Features:**\n\n- **Natural Language Design**: Describe applications in plain English to generate visual interfaces tailored to your specifications.[4]\n- **Image/Wireframe Upload**: Convert design sketches, screenshots, or wireframes into digital UIs.[4]\n- **AI-Native Canvas**: An infinite canvas for ideation through working prototypes with a new design agent that tracks progress across multiple ideas.[2]\n- **\"Vibe Design\"**: Start with business objectives and user experience goals rather than wireframes.[2]\n- **Interactive Prototypes**: Transform static designs into interactive prototypes instantly with the ability to preview user journeys by clicking \"Play.\"[2]\n- **Design Systems (DESIGN.md)**: Extract design systems from URLs or use markdown files to export/import design rules to other tools.[2]\n- **Export Options**: Export to Figma or as front-end code.[4]\n- **Voice Collaboration**: Use voice commands for real-time design critiques.[2]\n\n**Access**: Available at **stitch.withgoogle.com** and is currently free to use.[5]",
      "title": "Web search results"
    }
  ],
  "rejected": false
}

Here’s what I did and what you have.

### Stitch MCP
There is **no** MCP tool named “open project.” The read equivalent is **`get_project`**, which I called with the full resource name:

- **`name`:** `projects/18386357471434296476`

That **loaded the project** successfully from Stitch.

### Project summary
| Field | Value |
|--------|--------|
| **Title** | Search |
| **Resource name** | `projects/18386357471434296476` |
| **Visibility** | `PUBLIC` |
| **Type** | `TEXT_TO_UI_PRO` |
| **Device** | `MOBILE` |
| **Your role** | `READER` (from `metadata`) |
| **Created** | 2026-03-30T05:46:29Z |
| **Updated** | 2026-03-30T06:21:21Z |
| **Screens / instances** | Multiple `screenInstances` on the canvas (several marked `hidden: true`), plus one `DESIGN_SYSTEM_INSTANCE` |

The response also includes **design theme**, **thumbnail**, and the long **design system markdown** (same “Cinematic Curator” content as before).

### Opening in the browser
The MCP **cannot** start your browser or focus the Stitch UI. To open it yourself, go to **[Stitch – Projects](https://stitch.withgoogle.com/projects)** while signed in; this project is **public**, so it should be reachable from there if Google lists it for your account.

### Next steps in MCP (optional)
If you want to drill into a specific frame, say which **screen id** you care about (from `screenInstances[].id`), and we can use **`get_screen`** or **`list_screens`** with the right `projects/.../screens/...` name—still **no changes** to your app repo.

### Resource names (for `get_screen` / other tools)

Each screen’s full name is `projects/18386357471434296476/screens/<screenId>` (use the ID column above).

### Assets per screen

For every row, the API includes:

- **`screenshot`** – `downloadUrl` (image preview)
- **`htmlCode`** – `downloadUrl` (HTML export), `mimeType: text/html`

Those URLs are **signed / session-style Google links**; treat them as sensitive and avoid logging them in shared places.
